title: UNESCO celebrates 30 years of the “Safeguarding the Bayon ...
highlights: Constructed between the 12th and 13th century, the Bayon Temple constituted the culmination of the monumental  ...    The temple lays at the heart of the royal city of Angkor Thom, whose planimetry represents a sacred mandala: due to its location, and its mountain-like aspect, the Bayon Temple was envisioned as an earthly representation of Mount Meru, the  ...    The present-day labyrinthic structure of the site, with its complex set of galleries, towers, terraces, and pavilions, is a testament to its long and often troubled history. Born as a Buddhist site, the Bayon was then transformed into a Hindu temple, under the rule of King Jayavarman VIII, and stripped of great part of its religious imagery, only to be later reconverted into a Buddhist place of worship, a role which it still holds today. The alterations and additions carried out through the decades gave shape to the unique blend of Hindu and Buddhist
